EXAMPLES IN SENTENCES: The addition of the word 'as' to another word to intensify the adjective is so common in NZ that  I have left thos in  where it is commnly used.  Logically they should be removed in the same way I have consolidated the -ed and -ing variations but I suspect the young users will find it useful to include it on its own line.   HOWEVER after line 550 I have tended to delete thm if they are not very different from the base term

Where a normal word is defined here just as it would be in a dictionary and a competent English speaker may have heard it before then it has been deleted . However some words of this type are very unusual in normal conversation so I have included them in this list.

The AI suggested a large number of terms they I do not recognise ANd think they have no linguistic foundation. They may be extracted from a game or media character. I think they are likely only  used by niche groups or very few people if they are used at all so I have deleted them. I have left in words that have some logic such as being a phoenetic variation or a logical pun on a correct word .Some of theses I have never heard but I can see that they could logically exist.. If a user comes across the type of word that I have deleted and can veryify they are in common use them insert them.  I have left some in with ??? in the NOTES as I am uncertain.

I have noticed what I think are slang words in the example sentences or the listed word suggests another, that are not in the master list.  I have inserted many in the correct place but I am sure there are many others.  This will commonly apply where a term can be used in many ways with several different meanings - the term FUCK is a classic example.
Would it be useful to specify the part of sceech of the term.  eg Noun, Verb, Adjective etc.
